annotation.token: Intelligence Community Technical Specification XML Data Encoding Specification for Trusted Data Format (IC-TDF.XML)
W3C XML Schema for the XML Data Encoding Specification for the intelligence version of Trusted Data Format (IC-TDF.XML).
This XML Schema file is one component of the XML Data Encoding Specification (DES). Please see the document titled
XML Data Encoding Specification for Intelligence Trusted Data Format
for a complete description of the encoding as well as list of all components.

It is envisioned that this schema or its components, as well as other parts of the DES may be overridden for localized implementations. Therefore, permission to use, copy, modify and distribute this XML Schema and the other parts of the DES for any purpose is hereby granted in perpetuity.

Please reference the preceding two paragraphs in all copies or variations. The developers make no representation about the suitability of the schema or DES for any purpose. It is provided "as is" without expressed or implied warranty.
If you modify this XML Schema in any way label your schema as a variant of IC-TDF.XML.
Please direct all questions, bug reports,or suggestions for changes to the points of contact identified in the document referenced above.
The root elements for a TDF instance are either:
Office of the Director of National Intelligence Intelligence Community Chief Information Officer

The schema change logs will no longer be maintained as of the 2017-JUL release. The existing change logs will only serve as legacy information. For changes to schema as of and after 2017-JUL, reference the change history in the DES.
Changed assertion group to be 1-to-1 instead of 1-to-Many for TDO which was missed previously.
Enforced DESVersion with a pattern instead of a fixed value to allow extension versions
Updated to support Revision Recall Handling Assertion.
Enforcing ordinger so Handling assertions comes first:
Changed cardinality of assertion group to be 1-to-1 instead of 1-to-Many for TDC
Changed cardinality of HandlingAssertions in assertionGroup to be 1-to-Many from 1-to-1
Changed cardinality of HandlingAssertions in assertionGroup to be 0-to-Many from 1-to-1
Added scopes [DESC_TDO], [DESC_PAYL] and [TDC_MEMBER] for use within TDC Assertions
Added support for multiple binding by changing @max to "unbounded" on Binding element in the BindingGroup
The root element of a Trusted Data Collection.
The version number of the DES
The version number of the DES.

A specific type of assertion designed to be used for access, rights, and handling instructions. It is expected that handling instructions should never have metadata about themselves and they should never be encrypted. Therefore, unlike regular assertions, handling assertions do not support statement metadata or encryption.

Used to indicate if the statement metadata applies to encrypted or unencrypted data. If a TDO payload or assertion statement is encrypted, there are in fact two potentially different markings needed for decision making, analysis and querying, one describing the handling required for the encrypted blob, and the other for the handling required for the unencrypted (and in effect external) state. In cases where statements and/or payloads are encrypted, allow handling assertions and statement metadata elements to indicate whether their marks apply to the encrypted blob state vs. actual data by using an attribute appliesToState
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
The grouping of objects to which the assertion applies. Please see the "Assertion Scopes" section in the DES document for more information.
Intended for access, rights, and/or handling instructions that apply to the scope of the assertion.
Contains information needed to express, understand, and/or cryptographically validate the binding of the objects that belong to the scope of the assertion.
Information pertaining to the person or entity that performed the signing/binding and their credentials.
The distinguished name of the authority that issued the credentials to the subject. Refer to RFC 5280 for more information.
The unique serial number of the credentials given to the subject by the issuer. Refer to RFC 5280 for more information.
The distinguished name of the person or entity who is doing the signing. Refer to RFC 5280 for more information.
Stores the value of the Signature over the bound entities.

Used to indicate whether or not to include element StatementMetadata when referencing an Assertion. In the case of signatures and binding, this attribute indicates whether or not the statement metadata is covered by the signature or binding. If not, it cannot be cryptographically verified and should be considered informative only. IncludesStatementMetadata should never be set on SignatureValue if there is a boundValueList, because the BoundValue elements in the list each have their own explicit includesStatementMetadata attribute.

A URI that provides guidance on how to format the included values such as whitespace, attributes, and child nodes in a universally consistent manner. The normalization method is essential to prevent formatting such as whitespace and order from interfering with the validation of the cryptographic integrity of data.
The algorithm or pattern used by the signature. The permissible values are defined in the Controlled Value Enumeration: CVEnumTDFSignatureAlgorithm.xml

BoundValueList is a container of bound value references that point to the elements that are included in a cryptographic binding. The intent of the BoundValueList is to allow granular control over the scope of the binding signature. In the future, when BoundValueList is present, the SignatureValue will be calculated over the normalized value of the BoundValueList using the normalization method denoted in the Binding/SignatureValue/@normalizationMethod attribute.
A bound value is a reference that points to an element that is included in a cryptographic binding. A bound value is only meaningful in the context of a BoundValueList.
The hash algorithm used in the cryptographic binding.
A reference to a unique local identifier.

Used to indicate whether or not to include element StatementMetadata when referencing an Assertion. In the case of signatures and binding, this attribute indicates whether or not the statement metadata is covered by the signature or binding. If not, it cannot be cryptographically verified and should be considered informative only. IncludesStatementMetadata should never be set on SignatureValue if there is a boundValueList, because the BoundValue elements in the list each have their own explicit includesStatementMetadata attribute.

A URI that provides guidance on how to format the included values such as whitespace, attributes, and child nodes in a universally consistent manner. The normalization method is essential to prevent formatting such as whitespace and order from interfering with the validation of the cryptographic integrity of data.
Contains information needed to express, understand, and/or validate the informative (non-cryptographic) binding of the objects that belong to the scope of the assertion.
A reference to a unique local identifier.

Used to indicate whether or not to include element StatementMetadata when referencing an Assertion. In the case of signatures and binding, this attribute indicates whether or not the statement metadata is covered by the signature or binding. If not, it cannot be cryptographically verified and should be considered informative only. IncludesStatementMetadata should never be set on SignatureValue if there is a boundValueList, because the BoundValue elements in the list each have their own explicit includesStatementMetadata attribute.

Used to express metadata about the objects expressed in the scope attribute of the assertion. An assertion also supports metadata about the assertion statement for the purposes of indicating any handling instructions pertinent to the statement itself. Also supports encrypted statements and binding the statement with objects in its scope.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
The grouping of objects to which the assertion applies. Please see the "Assertion Scopes" section in the DES document for more information.

The logical grouping to which the assertion belongs. The Assertion type attribute is intended to provide additional context, allowing various systems to pre-determine relevance of assertions without parsing or reading all of the assertions. Type might include categorizations such as discovery, mission, or task order to allow various systems to determine which assertions are relevant for them to parse.

Intended for access, rights, handling or other metadata that applies to the assertion statement. Use EDH security options whenever an assertion already has a unique enterprise identifier or is intended for potential extraction and should be able stand on it's own as a separate referenceable object. Use arh security only when assertions are not intended to be extracted and do not require enterprise identifiers.

Used to indicate if the statement metadata applies to encrypted or unencrypted data. If a TDO payload or assertion statement is encrypted, there are in fact two potentially different markings needed for decision making, analysis and querying, one describing the handling required for the encrypted blob, and the other for the handling required for the unencrypted (and in effect external) state. In cases where statements and/or payloads are encrypted, allow handling assertions and statement metadata elements to indicate whether their marks apply to the encrypted blob state vs. actual data by using an attribute appliesToState.

Top level element for holding information related to the encryption of an assertion or payload. Multiple child KeyAccess and/or EncryptionMethod elements represent onion or layered encryption. In this case, the first child represents the outermost layer of encryption.
The sequence number of the encryption info.
Contains information pertaining to the key for which the application value(s) was/were encrypted and/or that is necessary for decryption.
Stores retrieval information for keys stored in remote locations.
The protocol related to retrieving remote keys.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.
Contains the key necessary for decryption in an encrypted state with information pertaining to the method in which the key was encrypted.
The value of the decription key.
Used to indicated that the key is based on a password.
The algorithm related to the password key.
Stores the alias that references a key that has been previously shared.
An alias for the shared key.
The store for the shared key.
Contains the key necessary for decryption.
Contains the key necessary for decryption in an encrypted state with information pertaining to the method in which the key was encrypted.
Contains information pertaining to the methods for which the applicable value(s) was/were encrypted. (i.e. SHA256)
Used to express the encryption algorithm utilized
The size of the key used for encryption expressed as an integer.

The name of the primary encoding format of the key. The primary encoding format is named in terms of the appropriate ASN.1 data format, if an ASN.1 specification for the key exists. For example, the name of the ASN.1 data format for public keys is SubjectPublicKeyInfo, as defined by the X.509 standard; in this case, the returned format is "X.509". Similarly, the name of the ASN.1 data format for private keys is PrivateKeyInfo, as defined by the PKCS #8 standard; in this case, the returned format is "PKCS#8".
Used to express the Initialization Vector (IV) used by block cipher modes of operation.
Used to express the Optimal Asymmetric Encryption Padding (OAEP) scheme
Used to express the Hash function used with the Optimal Asymmetric Encryption Padding (OAEP) scheme.
Used to express the Mask Generation Function used with the Optimal Asymmetric Encryption Padding (OAEP) scheme.
Used to express the Tweak used by various Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) schemes.
Used to express the Nonce used by various Offset Codebook (OCB) mode schemes.
Used to express the Additional Authentication Data (AAD) for Galois Counter Mode (GCM) of block cipher algorithms.
A cryptographic checksum on data that is designed to reveal both accidental errors and the intentional modification of the data in Galois Counter Mode (GCM) of block cipher algorithms.
Intended for textual statement content encoded as a string. Perhaps the contents of a text file.
This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
Intended for holding base64binary statement values such as a file or other binary encoded data.
This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in
Used to reference statements that are not embedded in the TDO but stored in a remote/external location.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in
The total blocks of a hash.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.
A smaller piece/block of the ReferenceValue that can be used to support transport (AKA "chunking") across a CDS.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.
Intended for structured content encoded in the same data encoding of the encapsulating Assertion (i.e. If the encoded format is XML this is intended for XML statements).

group.ref: ANY element from ANY namespace OTHER than 'urn:us:gov:ic:tdf'

annotation.token: This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
The root element of a Trusted Data Object. A Trusted Data Collection may contain many Trusted Data Objects.
The version number of the DES
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
The version number of the DES.
Intended for textual value content encoded as a string. Perhaps the contents of a text file.
This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
Intended for holding base64binary values such as a file or other binary data.
This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in
Used to reference payloads that are not embedded in the TDO but stored in a remote/external location.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in
The total blocks of a hash.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.
Intended for structured content encoded in the same data encoding of the encapsulating TDO (i.e. If the encoded format is XML this is intended for XML statements).

group.ref: ANY element from ANY namespace OTHER than 'urn:us:gov:ic:tdf'

annotation.token: This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
A list of Trusted Data Collections.
The version number of the DES
The version number of the DES.
The group of elements intended for access, rights, and/or handling instructions that apply to the scope of the assertion.

Used to indicate if the statement metadata applies to encrypted or unencrypted data. If a TDO payload or assertion statement is encrypted, there are in fact two potentially different markings needed for decision making, analysis and querying, one describing the handling required for the encrypted blob, and the other for the handling required for the unencrypted (and in effect external) state. In cases where statements and/or payloads are encrypted, allow handling assertions and statement metadata elements to indicate whether their marks apply to the encrypted blob state vs. actual data by using an attribute appliesToState
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
The grouping of objects to which the assertion applies. Please see the "Assertion Scopes" section in the DES document for more information.
A list of assertions designed to be used for access, rights, and handling instructions.
A list of the objects that belong to the scope of the assertion.
Stores the value of the Signature over the bound entities.

Used to indicate whether or not to include element StatementMetadata when referencing an Assertion. In the case of signatures and binding, this attribute indicates whether or not the statement metadata is covered by the signature or binding. If not, it cannot be cryptographically verified and should be considered informative only. IncludesStatementMetadata should never be set on SignatureValue if there is a boundValueList, because the BoundValue elements in the list each have their own explicit includesStatementMetadata attribute.

A URI that provides guidance on how to format the included values such as whitespace, attributes, and child nodes in a universally consistent manner. The normalization method is essential to prevent formatting such as whitespace and order from interfering with the validation of the cryptographic integrity of data.
The algorithm or pattern used by the signature. The permissible values are defined in the Controlled Value Enumeration: CVEnumTDFSignatureAlgorithm.xml

BoundValueList is a container of bound value references that point to the elements that are included in a cryptographic binding. The intent of the BoundValueList is to allow granular control over the scope of the binding signature. In the future, when BoundValueList is present, the SignatureValue will be calculated over the normalized value of the BoundValueList using the normalization method denoted in the Binding/SignatureValue/@normalizationMethod attribute.
A bound value is a reference that points to an element that is included in a cryptographic binding. A bound value is only meaningful in the context of a BoundValueList.
The hash algorithm used in the cryptographic binding.
A reference to a unique local identifier.

Used to indicate whether or not to include element StatementMetadata when referencing an Assertion. In the case of signatures and binding, this attribute indicates whether or not the statement metadata is covered by the signature or binding. If not, it cannot be cryptographically verified and should be considered informative only. IncludesStatementMetadata should never be set on SignatureValue if there is a boundValueList, because the BoundValue elements in the list each have their own explicit includesStatementMetadata attribute.

A URI that provides guidance on how to format the included values such as whitespace, attributes, and child nodes in a universally consistent manner. The normalization method is essential to prevent formatting such as whitespace and order from interfering with the validation of the cryptographic integrity of data.
A reference to a unique local identifier.

Used to indicate whether or not to include element StatementMetadata when referencing an Assertion. In the case of signatures and binding, this attribute indicates whether or not the statement metadata is covered by the signature or binding. If not, it cannot be cryptographically verified and should be considered informative only. IncludesStatementMetadata should never be set on SignatureValue if there is a boundValueList, because the BoundValue elements in the list each have their own explicit includesStatementMetadata attribute.
An attribute for expressing the AssertionType of an object Used to express metadata about the objects expressed in the scope attribute of the assertion.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
The grouping of objects to which the assertion applies. Please see the "Assertion Scopes" section in the DES document for more information.

The logical grouping to which the assertion belongs. The Assertion type attribute is intended to provide additional context, allowing various systems to pre-determine relevance of assertions without parsing or reading all of the assertions. Type might include categorizations such as discovery, mission, or task order to allow various systems to determine which assertions are relevant for them to parse.
Used to indicate if the statement metadata applies to encrypted or unencrypted data.

Used to indicate if the statement metadata applies to encrypted or unencrypted data. If a TDO payload or assertion statement is encrypted, there are in fact two potentially different markings needed for decision making, analysis and querying, one describing the handling required for the encrypted blob, and the other for the handling required for the unencrypted (and in effect external) state. In cases where statements and/or payloads are encrypted, allow handling assertions and statement metadata elements to indicate whether their marks apply to the encrypted blob state vs. actual data by using an attribute appliesToState.
Stores the value of the key stored in remote locations.
A list for retrieval information for keys stored in remote locations.
The protocol related to retrieving remote keys.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.
Stores the value of the key necessary for decryption in an encrypted state with information pertaining to the method in which the key was encrypted.
Stores the value of the key is based on a password.
The algorithm related to the password key.
A list of aliases for referencing a key that has been previously shared.
An alias for the shared key.
The store for the shared key.
Stores the value of the key necessary for decryption.
Stores the value of the key necessary for decryption in an encrypted state with information pertaining to the method in which the key was encrypted.
Used to express the encryption algorithm utilized
Intended for textual content encoded as a string.
This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
A type for holding base64binary values.
This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in RFC 4288.
Incorporates a value by reference to a URI where it can be found.

To support division of a value into smaller pieces for transport (AKA "chunking"), such as across a CDS, the body of the element may contain a list of ReferenceValueBlock elements. If so, each must have a URI to the block and an integer block number indicating the order in which the blocks can be re-assembled into the original payload. Block numbers must start at 1 and be sequential. When a list of ReferenceValueBlocks is used, a TotalHash element must be present and must have a totalBlocks attribute set to an integer indicating the number of such elements.

Not all systems will be willing or able to support unbounded lists of blocks. When tailoring maxOccurs here to reflect limitations imposed by a CDS or other implementation, that change should also be reflected in the definition of a BlockedHashGroup.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in
The total blocks of a hash.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.
A smaller piece/block of the ReferenceValue that can be used to support transport (AKA "chunking") across a CDS.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.

Intended for structured content encoded in the same data encoding of the encapsulating TDO (i.e. If the encoded format is XML this is intended for XML values). For signable StructuredValueType elements, it can be safer to declare namespaces locally to the section being signed to reduce risk in moving sections between documents. Explicit namespace declarations should be used and c14n11 normalization should be preferred when signing since c14n11 normalization does not perform any namespace re-writing and as a result, signed assertions can not be copied between documents unless the namespaces used are identical, or the assertion locally overrides them. Older c14n 1.0 has two approaches to namespace re-writing, either of which could in some circumstances break signatures when copying signed assertions between documents.

group.ref: ANY element from ANY namespace OTHER than 'urn:us:gov:ic:tdf'

annotation.token: This is the filename of the payload.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
A list of Trusted Data Objects.
The version number of the DES
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.
The version number of the DES.
The distinguished name of the person or entity who is doing the signing. Refer to RFC 5280 for more information.
The distinguished name of the authority that issued the credentials to the subject. Refer to RFC 5280 for more information.
The unique serial number of the credentials given to the subject by the issuer. Refer to RFC 5280 for more information.
The algorithm or pattern used by the signature. The permissible values are defined in the Controlled Value Enumeration: CVEnumTDFSignatureAlgorithm.xml

A URI that provides guidance on how to format the included values such as whitespace, attributes, and child nodes in a universally consistent manner. The normalization method is essential to prevent formatting such as whitespace and order from interfering with the validation of the cryptographic integrity of data.

Used to indicate whether or not to include element StatementMetadata when referencing an Assertion. In the case of signatures and binding, this attribute indicates whether or not the statement metadata is covered by the signature or binding. If not, it cannot be cryptographically verified and should be considered informative only. IncludesStatementMetadata should never be set on SignatureValue if there is a boundValueList, because the BoundValue elements in the list each have their own explicit includesStatementMetadata attribute.
A reference to a unique local identifier.
The hash algorithm used in the cryptographic binding.
(U) Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA) with a 160 bit message digest. SHA-1 is the most widely used of the existing SHA hash functions, and is employed in several widely used applications and protocols.
(U) Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA) with a 256 bit message digest.
(U) Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA) with a 384 bit message digest.
(U) Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA) with a 512 bit message digest.
The grouping of objects to which the assertion applies. Please see the "Assertion Scopes" section in the DES document for more information.
A unique local identifier used for binding and signing purposes. Not guaranteed to be unique across multiple TDC/TDOs but must be unique within a single instance of either.

Used to indicate if the statement metadata applies to encrypted or unencrypted data. If a TDO payload or assertion statement is encrypted, there are in fact two potentially different markings needed for decision making, analysis and querying, one describing the handling required for the encrypted blob, and the other for the handling required for the unencrypted (and in effect external) state. In cases where statements and/or payloads are encrypted, allow handling assertions and statement metadata elements to indicate whether their marks apply to the encrypted blob state vs. actual data by using an attribute appliesToState

Used to indicate if the statement metadata applies to encrypted or unencrypted data. If a TDO payload or assertion statement is encrypted, there are in fact two potentially different markings needed for decision making, analysis and querying, one describing the handling required for the encrypted blob, and the other for the handling required for the unencrypted (and in effect external) state. In cases where statements and/or payloads are encrypted, allow handling assertions and statement metadata elements to indicate whether their marks apply to the encrypted blob state vs. actual data by using an attribute appliesToState.
The protocol related to retrieving remote keys.
A uri expressing the location of the referenced material.
The algorithm related to the password key.
An alias for the shared key.
The store for the shared key.
Used to express the encryption algorithm utilized
The sequence number of the encryption info.
This is the filename of the payload.
Used to denote if contents are encrypted. When this optional attribute is absent, it is assumed to be false.
An attribute for expressing the mediaType of an object as defined in

The logical grouping to which the assertion belongs. The Assertion type attribute is intended to provide additional context, allowing various systems to pre-determine relevance of assertions without parsing or reading all of the assertions. Type might include categorizations such as discovery, mission, or task order to allow various systems to determine which assertions are relevant for them to parse.
The version number of the DES.
The group of possible Assertion elements in a TDO or TDC.
The group of elements that contains information needed to express, understand, and/or cryptographically validate the binding of the objects that belong to the scope of the assertion.
The group of elements used to express encryption information in an Assertion or a TDO.
The group of elements intended for contextual statement content encoded as a string.
The group of elements intended for textual payload content encoded as a string. Perhaps the contents of a text file.
